bead.title: Route --model to live LM Studio providers via discovery
bead.description:
When --model names a model not in the static catalog (e.g. qwen3.6-35b-a3b),
NormalizeRouteRequest treats it as an exact ModelPin. The candidate scoring
then picks whichever harness wins on profile — typically claude — which
cannot serve local models. The user must specify --harness lmstudio to
work around it.

The information needed to route correctly already exists: ddx agent check
discovers live LM Studio providers (vidar, bragi) and their advertised
model lists. But BuildCandidatePlans / evaluateCandidate never consults
live provider discovery when scoring candidates for a ModelPin. A candidate
with IsHTTPProvider=true whose discovered model list includes the pinned
model should score as viable; candidates that cannot serve the pin should
be rejected.

This is the model→provider discovery gap referenced in ddx-1cfce460. The
PATH fix landed but the discovery wiring did not.
bead.acceptance:
1. ddx work --model qwen3.6-35b-a3b routes to the lmstudio harness
   (or agent harness backed by a provider advertising the model) without
   requiring --harness or --provider.
2. When no discovered provider advertises the pinned model, routing falls
   back to the current behavior (exact pin on best-scoring harness).
3. Unit test: given a discovered provider advertising model X and a
   RouteRequest with ModelPin=X, the lmstudio candidate is viable and
   ranked above harnesses that cannot serve the model.
4. cd cli && go test ./... && go build .
